steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence opens with the participial phrase "Having 9,721 feet of prominence," which must be immediately followed by the noun it modifies — the thing that actually possesses that topographical prominence. Any other subject creates a dangling modifier.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Pico do Ramelau': the mountain itself has the prominence; the modifier correctly attaches to it. ✓
  • Choice B — 'the list of mountains': a list cannot possess topographical prominence. Dangling modifier. ✗
  • Choice C — 'geographers': people do not have 9,721 feet of prominence. Dangling modifier. ✗
  • Choice D — 'the ranking': an abstract ranking cannot possess physical prominence. Dangling modifier. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
